hero.title: Sip history. Stay a while.
hero.lead: Set in a historic building of Kinomoto, we run a sake-forward bar "Ikuhi" and revive a former ryokan as "Nakaya Ryokan."

bar.title: Sake Bar "Ikuhi"
bar.body: A bar where sake takes center stage. Kinomoto is home to two of the oldest breweries in Japan, and sake culture has long flourished here. We feature Japanese sake alongside a range of domestically produced spirits and liqueurs. Dishes are designed to pair with drinks—meat, fish, and vegetables—with a small menu that changes every few days.
bar.badge1Body: A broad selection focused on local labels.

stay.title: Stay "Nakaya Ryokan"
stay.fac2Body: A shared space that sparks conversation.
stay.fac3Body: Catch your breath in a seasonal courtyard.

history.title: HISTORY
history.body: Kinomoto thrived as a post town where the Hokkokukaidō and its branch route intersected, with travelers coming from Kyoto, Nagoya, and Edo. The town still retains this atmosphere and protects its streetscape. The building that houses Ikuhi operated as a ryokan until the Taishō era. It later became a catering shop, kimono store, stationery shop, and a cram school before falling vacant about 15 years ago.

concept.title: CONCEPT
concept.body: There is currently no lodging on the Hokkokukaidō. By bringing this former ryokan back to life, we hope to create a place where travelers and townspeople meet. We want the world to discover beautiful, historic Kinomoto and help the town regain its bustle as a post station. We also hope locals will recognize the value of these unique Hokko buildings, be motivated to revitalize vacant houses, and join us in rebuilding the town.

access.title: Access
access.carValue: Approx. X min from Kinomoto IC, Hokuriku Expressway (TBD)
access.mapNote: * An accurate map will be added once the address is confirmed.
access.trainBody: Take the JR Hokuriku Line to Kinomoto Station, then walk approx. 8 minutes.
access.carBody: Approx. 15 minutes from Kinomoto IC on the Hokuriku Expressway.
access.publicBody: Kinomoto is a historic post town in the Kohoku region of northern Shiga Prefecture. Nearby attractions include Shizugatake, Lake Yogo, and Chikubushima Island.

cta.title: Follow us on social
cta.body: Stay up to date on Instagram, Facebook, and LINE.

gallery.title: Photo Gallery
gallery.lead: Experience the transformation: vacant → under construction → completed.

rooms.title: Our Rooms
rooms.lead: Nakaya Ryokan offers three rooms. Each space preserves the character of the historic building while providing modern comfort.
rooms.room01Desc: A calm Japanese-Western room with one king-size bed. The window looks out over the quiet streets of Kinomoto—a perfect room for a relaxed, unhurried stay.
rooms.room02Desc: A spacious room with two king-size beds, ideal for families or small groups. The design makes the most of the historic exposed beams.
rooms.room03Desc: A room with two double beds, facing the inner courtyard. Enjoy the changing scenery of each season from a tranquil Japanese space.

reservation.title: Reservations
reservation.lead: Booking directly through our official site gives you the best rate. Send us a reservation request below and we will get back to you within 2 business days.
